name: HubSpot MCP Server
what_it_does: HubSpot MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with HubSpot's CRM and marketing platform — managing contacts, companies, deals, and tickets; querying engagement history; automating sales workflows; sending emails; and integrating HubSpot CRM data into agent-driven sales and marketing automation.
use_cases: Creating and updating contacts and companies from sales agents
Managing deal pipelines and stages from CRM automation agents
Querying customer engagement history from customer success agents
Creating and managing support tickets from service agents
Sending marketing emails and sequences from outreach agents
Syncing CRM data with external systems from integration agents
not_for: Teams using Salesforce, Pipedrive, or another CRM as primary system
Large enterprise CRM with complex customizations (Salesforce better fit)
best_when: An agent needs to read or write HubSpot CRM data for sales automation, customer success, or marketing workflows.
avoid_when: You're using Salesforce, Pipedrive, or another CRM — or if you need HubSpot's marketing automation platform specifically.

auth.notes: HubSpot private app access tokens (preferred) or legacy API key. Private apps support OAuth-like scopes. OAuth 2.0 for user-facing integrations. Private app tokens are scoped to specific HubSpot objects.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free CRM: unlimited contacts, basic features, unlimited users
pricing.notes: Generous free CRM tier with API access. Paid tiers add marketing automation, sequences, advanced reporting. API access included across all tiers. MCP server is community open source.

performance.rate_limits: 100 req/10s for most APIs; burst of 110 req/10s allowed

known_agent_gotchas: HubSpot object IDs (hs_object_id) vs custom identifiers — always use internal IDs for API calls
Rate limits apply per portal (account) — 100 req/10s is shared across all API calls
Private app tokens expire after 30 days if not refreshed (newer tokens are non-expiring)
HubSpot v1 vs v2 vs v3 API endpoints coexist — use v3 for new integrations
Association types between objects require correct association category and type IDs
CRM properties differ by object type — contact vs company vs deal have different schema
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Private app tokens with object-level scopes. OAuth 2.0 for user-facing apps. SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR. Community MCP server — review coverage before production use.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Private app tokens with object-level scopes. OAuth 2.0 available. SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR. Community MCP server.
